title: [InstCombine] Fold `sext(A < B) + zext(A > B)` into `ucmp/scmp(A, B)`

tests.source_program:
define i16 @unsigned_cmp_test(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %lt = icmp ult i64 %a, %b
  %lt16 = sext i1 %lt to i16
  %gt = icmp ugt i64 %a, %b
  %gt16 = zext i1 %gt to i16
  %r = add i16 %lt16, %gt16
  ret i16 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on signed comparisons (scmp) but doesn't explicitly detail unsigned comparison handling (ucmp). Known tests primarily demonstrate signed predicates (slt/sgt), not unsigned (ult/ugt).

alive2.src:
define i16 @unsigned_cmp_test(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %lt = icmp ult i64 %a, %b
  %lt16 = sext i1 %lt to i16
  %gt = icmp ugt i64 %a, %b
  %gt16 = zext i1 %gt to i16
  %r = add i16 %lt16, %gt16
  ret i16 %r
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i16 @unsigned_cmp_test(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
#0:
  %lt = icmp ult i64 %a, %b
  %lt16 = sext i1 %lt to i16
  %gt = icmp ugt i64 %a, %b
  %gt16 = zext i1 %gt to i16
  %r = add i16 %lt16, %gt16
  ret i16 %r
}
=>
define i16 @unsigned_cmp_test(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
#0:
  %r = ucmp i16 i64 %a, %b
  %#range_0_%r = !range i16 %r, i16 65535, i16 2
  ret i16 %#range_0_%r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
define i64 @i64_width_test(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %lt = icmp slt i64 %a, %b
  %lt64 = sext i1 %lt to i64
  %gt = icmp sgt i64 %a, %b
  %gt64 = zext i1 %gt to i64
  %r = add i64 %lt64, %gt64
  ret i64 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't specify which integer widths are supported. Known tests use i8/i32 result types, but the pattern should work for i64 destination types as well.

alive2.src:
define i64 @i64_width_test(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %lt = icmp slt i64 %a, %b
  %lt64 = sext i1 %lt to i64
  %gt = icmp sgt i64 %a, %b
  %gt64 = zext i1 %gt to i64
  %r = add i64 %lt64, %gt64
  ret i64 %r
}

  %gt = icmp sgt i64 %a, %b
  %gt64 = zext i1 %gt to i64
  %r = add i64 %lt64, %gt64
  ret i64 %r
}

tests.source_program:
define <4 x i32> @wide_vector_test(<4 x i64> %a, <4 x i64> %b) {
  %lt = icmp slt <4 x i64> %a, %b
  %ltvec = sext <4 x i1> %lt to <4 x i32>
  %gt = icmp sgt <4 x i64> %a, %b
  %gtvec = zext <4 x i1> %gt to <4 x i32>
  %r = add <4 x i32> %ltvec, %gtvec
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions vector support but known tests only show <2 x i8>. This tests wider vectors (<4 x i32>) with different source/destination element type combinations not covered in examples.

alive2.src:
define <4 x i32> @wide_vector_test(<4 x i64> %a, <4 x i64> %b) {
  %lt = icmp slt <4 x i64> %a, %b
  %ltvec = sext <4 x i1> %lt to <4 x i32>
  %gt = icmp sgt <4 x i64> %a, %b
  %gtvec = zext <4 x i1> %gt to <4 x i32>
  %r = add <4 x i32> %ltvec, %gtvec
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}

  %ltvec = sext <4 x i1> %lt to <4 x i32>
  %gt = icmp sgt <4 x i64> %a, %b
  %gtvec = zext <4 x i1> %gt to <4 x i32>
  %r = add <4 x i32> %ltvec, %gtvec
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
=>
define <4 x i32> @wide_vector_test(<4 x i64> %a, <4 x i64> %b) {
#0:
  %r = scmp <4 x i32> <4 x i64> %a, %b
  %#range_0_%r = !range <4 x i32> %r, i32 4294967295, i32 2
  ret <4 x i32> %#range_0_%r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
